message:
fix(wal,vdbe): add linear scan fallback for partial page index + fix temp register lifetime

WAL adapter (wal_adapter.rs):
- The capped page index (PAGE_INDEX_MAX_ENTRIES) previously silently dropped
  pages once the HashMap was full, causing read_page to incorrectly report
  pages as absent from the WAL. Added an `index_is_partial` flag that tracks
  when the cap is hit, and a `scan_backwards_for_page` fallback that performs
  a reverse linear scan of committed frames when a HashMap miss cannot be
  trusted.
- Added `page_index_cap` field (configurable in tests) so the partial-index
  fallback path can be exercised without writing 64K+ frames.
- Replaced unicode arrows (→) with ASCII (->) in doc comments for
  consistent rendering across terminals and editors.

VDBE codegen (codegen.rs):
- Moved `free_temp(r_saw_null)` after the done_label resolution in
  emit_in_probe_expr so the temporary register is not freed while still
  reachable through the null_label branch. Previously, freeing it before
  the null_label code could cause the register allocator to reuse it for
  an unrelated purpose while the NULL path still reads from it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
